title: Hot Cue Setting & Recall
description: Set cue points at key moments in a track and recall them instantly — your map through the music.

steps.instruction: Watch: the track plays and reaches the first downbeat. Hot Cue 1 is set by pressing the cue button.
Watch: the track continues playing to a second key moment — the breakdown. Hot Cue 2 is set there.
Watch: pressing Cue 1 instantly jumps back to the first downbeat. Hot cues are instant bookmarks.

phases.instruction: Think of hot cues as bookmarks in a book. You can jump instantly to the intro, the drop, the breakdown, or the outro without scrubbing. Set them once, use them every time you play that track.

steps.instruction: Play a track on Deck A. Let it run until you hear the first strong downbeat — the beginning of a musical phrase.
At the downbeat, press Cue on Deck A to mark this position. You have just set a cue point.
Let the track continue playing for another 16 bars. Listen for the next key section.
Now press Cue again to jump back to your saved point instantly. Notice how it snaps right back to the downbeat.
Press Play to start from your cue point. The track resumes from the exact downbeat you marked.
Practice the Cue → Play workflow twice more. Cue to return to the start, Play to launch from that position.
